agents.cancel_confirm_desc: Are you sure you want to cancel {label}?
agents.title: Agents ({count})
app.title: SoulFlow Dashboard
cfg.agentBackend.claudeBackend.desc: Claude agent execution mode: CLI wrapper or native SDK
cfg.agentBackend.codexBackend.desc: Codex agent execution mode: CLI wrapper or app server
cfg.agentLoopMaxTurns.desc: Maximum turns per agent conversation before auto-stop
cfg.channel.approvalReactionEnabled.desc: Allow users to approve actions via emoji reactions
cfg.channel.autoReply.desc: Automatically process and reply to incoming messages
cfg.channel.controlReactionEnabled.desc: Allow stop/cancel actions via emoji reactions
cfg.channel.defaultAlias.desc: Agent alias used when no specific agent is mentioned
cfg.channel.dispatch.dlqEnabled.desc: Save permanently failed messages to Dead Letter Queue
cfg.channel.dispatch.dlqPath.desc: SQLite file path for DLQ storage
cfg.channel.dispatch.inlineRetries.desc: Immediate retries before entering exponential backoff
cfg.channel.dispatch.retryBaseMs.desc: Base delay for exponential backoff calculation
cfg.channel.dispatch.retryJitterMs.desc: Random jitter added to retry delays to avoid thundering herd
cfg.channel.dispatch.retryMax.desc: Maximum retry attempts with exponential backoff
cfg.channel.grouping.enabled.desc: Combine multiple rapid responses into a single message to reduce spam
cfg.channel.grouping.maxMessages.desc: Maximum messages to group; flush immediately when reached
cfg.channel.grouping.windowMs.desc: Time window to collect messages before grouping and sending
cfg.channel.inboundConcurrency.desc: Max concurrent inbound messages being processed
cfg.channel.outboundDedupe.maxSize.desc: Maximum fingerprints stored in dedupe cache
cfg.channel.outboundDedupe.ttlMs.desc: Window for detecting duplicate outbound messages
cfg.channel.pollIntervalMs.desc: How often to check channels for new messages
cfg.channel.reactionActionTtlMs.desc: How long reaction-based actions remain valid
cfg.channel.readAckEnabled.desc: Add a reaction to confirm message was received
cfg.channel.readAckReaction.desc: Emoji reaction to add when a message is read
cfg.channel.readLimit.desc: Max messages to fetch per channel per poll cycle
cfg.channel.seenMaxSize.desc: Maximum entries in the seen-messages cache
cfg.channel.seenTtlMs.desc: How long to remember processed message IDs (dedup window)
cfg.channel.sessionHistoryMaxAgeMs.desc: Conversation context window; older messages are pruned
cfg.channel.streaming.enabled.desc: Stream partial responses while agent is thinking
cfg.channel.streaming.intervalMs.desc: How often to flush buffered text to the channel
cfg.channel.streaming.minChars.desc: Minimum characters before sending a stream update
cfg.channel.streaming.mode.desc: live: stream partial text, status: show status indicator then final response as new message
cfg.channel.streaming.suppressFinalAfter: Skip sending the final message if already streamed
cfg.cli.maxCaptureChars.desc: Maximum stdout/stderr capture size for CLI providers
cfg.cli.maxStreamStateChars.desc: Maximum buffer size for CLI stream state
cfg.dashboard.enabled.desc: Enable the web dashboard HTTP server
cfg.dashboard.host.desc: Bind address (0.0.0.0 for all interfaces, 127.0.0.1 for local only)
cfg.dashboard.portFallback.desc: Fallback to an ephemeral port if the configured port is in use
cfg.dashboard.publicUrl.desc: Publicly accessible base URL (e.g. https://dashboard.example.com). Used as OAuth redirect_uri base. Required when running behind a reverse proxy or with a custom domain
cfg.dataDir.desc: Path to store SQLite databases and persistent state
cfg.embedding.instanceId.desc: Provider instance for embeddings (registered in Providers with purpose=embedding). Empty = auto-select
cfg.embedding.model.desc: Model override for embeddings. Empty = use instance default model
cfg.logging.level.desc: Minimum log level for console output
cfg.memory.consolidation.archiveUsed.des: Delete daily memory entries after they have been consolidated
cfg.memory.consolidation.enabled.desc: Automatically compress conversation history into long-term memory
cfg.memory.consolidation.idleAfterMs.des: Wait time after last activity before triggering idle consolidation
cfg.memory.consolidation.intervalMs.desc: How often to run consolidation in cron mode (default: 24h)
cfg.memory.consolidation.trigger.desc: idle: run after session goes quiet; cron: run on fixed schedule
cfg.memory.consolidation.windowDays.desc: Number of recent daily memory days included in each consolidation run
cfg.memory.dailyInjectionDays.desc: Number of recent daily memory days to inject into the system prompt (0 = disabled)
cfg.memory.dailyInjectionMaxChars.desc: Character limit for daily memory injected into system prompt
cfg.mcp.enabled.desc: Enable MCP server integration for CLI providers
cfg.mcp.serverNames.desc: Comma-separated allowlist of MCP server names
cfg.mcp.serversFile.desc: Path to additional MCP servers JSON config file
cfg.mcp.startupTimeoutSec.desc: Default startup timeout for MCP servers (0 = none)
cfg.ops.bridgePumpEnabled.desc: Enable background inbound message polling bridge
cfg.ops.healthLogOnChange.desc: Only log health when state changes
cfg.orchestration.executorModel.desc: Model override for executor. Empty = use instance default
cfg.orchestration.executorProvider.desc: Provider instance for task execution (registered in Providers). Empty = auto-select
cfg.orchestration.maxToolResultChars.des: Truncate tool outputs beyond this length before sending to LLM
Maximum output tokens for orchestrator LLM calls
cfg.orchestration.orchestratorModel.desc: Model override for orchestrator. Empty = use instance default
cfg.orchestration.orchestratorProvider.d: Provider instance for orchestrator classification (registered in Providers). Empty = auto-select
cfg.orchestratorLlm.apiBase.desc: OpenAI-compatible API endpoint for orchestrator LLM inference
cfg.orchestratorLlm.autoStop.desc: Stop container when orchestrator shuts down
cfg.orchestratorLlm.container.desc: Name for the Ollama container instance
cfg.orchestratorLlm.enabled.desc: Enable local LLM runtime for classification tasks
cfg.orchestratorLlm.engine.desc: Container engine for running Ollama (auto detects available)
cfg.orchestratorLlm.gpuEnabled.desc: Enable GPU passthrough for container runtime
cfg.orchestratorLlm.model.desc: Model name to load in Ollama
cfg.orchestratorLlm.pullModel.desc: Automatically pull model on startup if not present
cfg.taskLoopMaxTurns.desc: Maximum turns for long-running background tasks
cfg.timezoneOffsetMin.desc: UTC offset in minutes. KST=540, JST=540, EST=-300
cfg.workspaceDir.desc: Working directory for agent file operations (read-only, set by cwd)

setup.persona_name_hint: The name your agent will use as its identity. Detailed persona setup is done in the first chat via BOOTSTRAP.
setup.step.complete.desc: Your orchestrator is ready. Redirecting to the dashboard...
setup.step.defaults.desc: Set the default executor and orchestrator providers.
setup.step.identity.desc: Set the agent persona name and bot alias.
setup.step.providers.desc: Choose which AI providers to enable. You can add API keys for providers that require them.
setup.subtitle: Configure your SoulFlow Orchestrator to get started.
setup.title: First-time Setup
skills.title: Skills ({count})
tool.ask_user.desc: Ask the user a question or request confirmation through the originating channel.
tool.chain.desc: Execute a pipeline of tools sequentially. Each step can reference previous outputs via $prev or $steps[N] templates. Set abort_on_error=false on a step to skip errors and continue.
tool.compress.desc: Compress/decompress files using gzip or brotli. Also supports base64 encoding of compressed data for API transfer.
tool.cron_shell.desc: Schedule shell commands with cron expressions. List, register, remove, or trigger jobs.
tool.cron.param.message: Message to send when job runs
tool.cron.param.name: Job name
tool.crypto.desc: Encrypt/decrypt data with AES-256-GCM. Sign/verify with RSA. Actions: encrypt, decrypt, sign, verify, generate_key.
tool.data_format.desc: Convert between data formats (JSON, CSV, YAML, TOML), query with JSONPath, validate, flatten/unflatten, and pretty-print.
tool.database.desc: Execute SQL queries against SQLite datasources. Supports SELECT, schema inspection, and DML with write policy.
tool.datetime.desc: Date/time operations: current time, add/subtract durations, diff, timezone conversion, business days, format, parse, day info, date range.
tool.diagram_render.desc: Render Mermaid diagrams to SVG or ASCII.
tool.diff.desc: Compare texts or files and produce unified diffs, or apply patches.
tool.docker.desc: Manage Docker containers: ps, run, stop, rm, logs, exec, images.
tool.edit_file.desc: Edit a file by replacing exact old text with new text.
tool.email.desc: Send emails via SMTP and receive via IMAP. Actions: send, list, read, search.
tool.email.param.subject: Subject
tool.embedding.desc: Generate text embeddings via provider API. Actions: embed, batch_embed, similarity.
tool.encoding.desc: Encode/decode (Base64, Hex, URL), generate hashes (SHA-256, SHA-512, MD5), and create UUIDs.
tool.eval.desc: Safely evaluate JavaScript expressions in a sandboxed context. Supports JSON context injection, multi-line code, and structured output.
tool.exec.desc: Execute a shell command and return stdout/stderr.
tool.filter.desc: Filter arrays/objects with expressions. Actions: where, find, reject, every, some, count.
tool.format.desc: Format values for display: numbers with locale/currency, bytes to KB/MB/GB, relative time, percentages, masking (email/phone/card), ordinals, pluralization, duration.
tool.git.desc: Execute Git operations: status, diff, log, commit, push, pull, branch, checkout, stash, tag.
tool.graphql.desc: Execute GraphQL queries and mutations. Supports variables, custom headers, and introspection.
tool.hash.desc: Compute hashes: MD5, SHA-256, SHA-512, HMAC. Actions: hash, hmac, verify.
tool.image.desc: Image operations: resize, crop, rotate, convert format, get info/metadata. Uses ImageMagick.
tool.jwt.desc: JWT operations: create, verify, decode tokens. Supports HS256, HS384, HS512, RS256.
tool.lookup.desc: Lookup reference data: HTTP status codes, MIME types, country codes, currency symbols. Also supports reverse lookup and listing all entries.
tool.markdown.desc: Markdown generation: JSON to table/list/checklist, HTML to markdown, table of contents from headings, badge/link/image generation, code block wrapping.
tool.math.desc: Math operations: evaluate expressions, unit conversion, financial calculations (compound interest, loan payment, ROI), rounding, percentage, gcd/lcm, factorial.
tool.network.desc: Network diagnostics: ping, dns lookup, port check, HTTP head, netstat.
tool.notification.desc: Send system notifications with level, title, and body.
tool.oauth_fetch.desc: Make an OAuth-authenticated API request (auto token injection and refresh).
tool.package_manager.desc: Manage packages: list, install, uninstall, audit, outdated. Auto-detects npm/pip/cargo.
tool.queue.desc: In-memory queue (FIFO, LIFO, Priority). Operations: enqueue, dequeue, peek, size, drain, list, clear, delete.
tool.read_file.desc: Read UTF-8 content from a file.
tool.regex.desc: Regex operations: match, match_all, replace, extract (named groups), split, and test.
tool.request_file.desc: Request a file upload from the user.
tool.runtime_admin.desc: Manage runtime: skills, dynamic tools, and MCP servers.
tool.screenshot.desc: Capture screenshots of web pages via headless browser. Supports full-page, viewport, and element-level capture.
tool.secret.param.name: Secret name
tool.send_file.desc: Send a local file to the current channel/chat.
tool.send_file.param.caption: Optional accompanying message
tool.set_ops.desc: Set operations on arrays: union, intersection, difference, symmetric_difference, is_subset, is_superset, equals, power_set, cartesian_product.
tool.spawn.desc: Spawn a headless subagent for a background task.
tool.spawn.param.label: Short label for tracking
tool.stats.desc: Statistical analysis on numeric data: summary, mean, median, stdev, percentile, histogram, correlation.
tool.system_info.desc: Collect system information: disk, memory, cpu, os, uptime, network interfaces.
tool.table.desc: Tabular data operations on JSON arrays: sort, filter, group_by, join, pivot, aggregate, distinct, slice, pluck, count_by.
tool.task_query.desc: Query the status of a spawned subagent/task.
tool.task_query.param.task_id: Task ID returned by spawn tool
tool.template_engine.desc: Render Mustache-style templates with variables, conditionals ({{#if}}), loops ({{#each}}), and helpers (upper, lower, json, default).
tool.text.desc: Text manipulation: case conversion, slugify, truncate, pad, word/char count, deduplicate lines, similarity (Levenshtein), join, wrap.
tool.transform.desc: Transform data structures. Actions: map, pick, omit, flatten, unflatten, group_by, sort_by, unique, zip.
tool.ttl_cache.desc: TTL-based key-value cache with LRU eviction. Operations: set, get, invalidate, has, keys, stats, clear.
tool.validator.desc: Validate data against JSON Schema or format rules (email, url, ip, date, uuid). Returns structured errors.
tool.vector_store.desc: CRUD operations on vector stores. Actions: create_collection, insert, query, delete, list_collections.
tool.web_auth.desc: Browser-based login: fill credentials, submit, persist session cookies.
tool.web_fetch.desc: Fetch a web page and return extracted text.
tool.web_form.desc: Auto-fill and submit web forms. Provide field-to-value mapping and submit selector.
tool.web_search.desc: Search the web and return results.
tool.web_table.desc: Extract structured table data from a web page. Returns array of row objects.
tool.webhook.desc: Register inbound HTTP webhook listeners. Actions: register, list, remove, get_recent.
tool.workflow.desc: IMPORTANT: Always use this tool to create/update/delete workflows. Never use write_file to create workflow YAML files directly.\n
tool.write_file.desc: Write UTF-8 content to a file (creates directories if needed).
tools.title: Tools ({count})
workflows.approval_message_hint: Message to send to approvers (use {{memory.*}} for dynamic values)
workflows.assert_error_message_hint: Message to show on validation failure
workflows.batch_array_field_hint: Path to array to process (e.g., memory.items)
workflows.batch_body_node_hint: Node ID to execute for each item
workflows.cancel_confirm_desc: Are you sure you want to cancel this running workflow? This cannot be undone.
workflows.filesystem_watch_trigger_desc: Trigger when files are added, changed, or deleted
workflows.channel_type_hint: Select a configured channel to listen to
workflows.condition_hint: JS expression. Available: memory.*, input.*, value, prev
workflows.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete?
workflows.context_template_hint: Reference outputs from previous phases/nodes. Drag output fields here.
workflows.cron_hint: Standard cron expression (min hour dom month dow). Example: 0 9 * * 1-5
workflows.cron_trigger_desc: Schedule workflow runs on a cron expression
workflows.webhook_path_hint: Path that receives the webhook. Example: /hooks/my-workflow
workflows.trigger_manual_hint: This workflow is triggered manually via API or dashboard. No parameters needed.
workflows.channel_chat_id_hint: Optional: filter to a specific chat ID
workflows.kanban_trigger_desc: Trigger on Kanban board card changes
workflows.kanban_trigger_board_id_hint: Select a board or type a board ID / scope:type:name
workflows.kanban_trigger_column_hint: Optional: filter by column ID (e.g., todo, in_progress, done)
workflows.fs_watch_path_hint: Relative path from workspace root (e.g., inbox/)
workflows.fs_watch_pattern_hint: Glob pattern (e.g., **/*.pdf). Leave empty to watch all files
workflows.fs_watch_batch_ms_hint: Wait time to group multiple file changes into one event
node.trigger_filesystem_watch.label: Filesystem Watch
workflows.db_query_hint: Use {{memory.*}} for template variables in queries
workflows.empty_canvas: Click "+ Phase" to start building your workflow
workflows.empty_canvas_dbl_click: Or double-click the canvas to add a Phase
workflows.empty_canvas_hint: Click + to add your first node
workflows.empty_hint: Use Quick Run above or create a workflow from a template.
workflows.escalation_message_hint: Message to send to escalation target
workflows.expression_hint: Access previous results via memory.prev.*, input.*, or value
workflows.filter_hint: JS expression per item. Available: item, index
workflows.gate_quorum_hint: Minimum completed sources needed to proceed
workflows.hitl_prompt_hint: Question to ask the user (use {{memory.*}} for dynamic values)
workflows.loop_array_hint: Field name from input containing the array to iterate
workflows.loop_body_hint: Comma-separated node IDs to execute per iteration
workflows.loop_max_hint: Maximum iterations to prevent infinite loops (1-10000)
workflows.max_turns_hint: Maximum conversation turns (each agent step counts as one turn)
workflows.merge_collect_hint: Collect all upstream outputs into an array
workflows.merge_first_hint: Continue as soon as any upstream branch completes
workflows.merge_wait_all_hint: Wait for all upstream branches to complete
workflows.mode_interactive_hint: Agents take turns; user can respond between iterations.
workflows.mode_loop_hint: Agent loops until condition met or max iterations reached.
workflows.mode_parallel_hint: All agents run concurrently — fastest execution.
workflows.objective_placeholder: Describe what this workflow should accomplish...
workflows.phase_node_desc: Agent execution phase with parallel/interactive modes
workflows.node_end_desc: Output node that distributes inputs to downstream output-based nodes
workflows.prompt_hint_interactive: Agent converses with user to build a spec. Use [ASK_USER] to ask, [SPEC_COMPLETE] to finish.
workflows.prompt_hint_parallel: Each agent runs independently. Write a prompt focused on a single task.
workflows.prompt_hint_sequential_loop: Runs with fresh context each iteration. Accumulate results and use [DONE] to terminate.
workflows.quick_run_label: Enter an objective to apply to the templates below, or run directly without a template.
workflows.quick_run_placeholder: Describe the goal of this workflow...
workflows.remove_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this item?
workflows.auto_approve_hint: Automatically approve Approval nodes and tool approval requests
workflows.retriever_top_k_hint: Maximum number of results to retrieve (1-100)
workflows.retry_max_delay_hint: Maximum interval between retries in milliseconds
workflows.retry_target_hint: Node ID to retry on failure
workflows.role_auto_prompt: System prompt is auto-generated from role preset
workflows.run_objective_placeholder: Enter objective / input for this Phase
workflows.set_hint: Array of {key, value} pairs. Use {{memory.*}} for references.
workflows.task_max_turns_hint: Maximum conversation turns for task execution (1-200)
workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.tool_invoke_id_hint: Select a tool or use {{memory.*}} for dynamic resolution
workflows.tool_invoke_params_hint: Parameters to pass to the tool (template supported)
workflows.tool_nodes_hint: Comma-separated node IDs the agent can call as tools
workflows.transform_hint: JS expression per item. Return transformed value. Available: item, index
workflows.vs_top_k_hint: Number of similar results to return (1-100)
workflows.webhook_trigger_desc: Start workflow from incoming HTTP webhook
workflows.yaml_parse_error: YAML parsing failed. Fix errors before switching tabs.
workspace.title: Workspace
node.barcode.label: Barcode
node.barcode.description: Generate and validate barcodes and QR codes
node.barcode.hint: Use action to specify operation: generate, validate_ean, validate_upc, etc.
node.changelog.label: Changelog
node.changelog.description: Analyze commit logs and generate changelog
node.changelog.hint: Specify repository URL and date range for changelog generation
node.circuit_breaker.label: Circuit Breaker
node.circuit_breaker.description: Implement circuit breaker pattern for fault tolerance
node.circuit_breaker.hint: Configure failure threshold, timeout, and recovery settings
node.code_diagram.label: Code Diagram
node.code_diagram.description: Generate UML diagrams and flowcharts from source code
node.code_diagram.hint: Provide source code file or URL to generate visual diagrams
node.cookie.label: Cookie
node.cookie.hint: Use for cookie manipulation in HTTP requests/responses
node.csv.label: CSV
node.csv.hint: Specify delimiter and quote character for custom CSV formats
node.data_mask.label: Data Mask
node.data_mask.description: Mask sensitive data (credit cards, SSN, emails, etc.)
node.data_mask.hint: Select data types to mask: card numbers, personal IDs, contact info
node.duration.label: Duration
node.duration.hint: Support ISO 8601 format and common units (ms, s, m, h, d)
node.ftp.label: FTP
node.ftp.description: Connect to FTP servers and transfer files
node.ftp.hint: Provide server address, credentials, and file paths
node.graph.label: Graph
node.graph.description: Implement graph algorithms (shortest path, traversal, etc.)
node.graph.hint: Define nodes and edges, select algorithm type
node.healthcheck.label: Health Check
node.healthcheck.description: Perform service health checks and monitoring
node.healthcheck.hint: Configure endpoints, timeout, and retry policies
node.html.label: HTML
node.html.description: Parse and extract data from HTML documents
node.html.hint: Use CSS selectors or XPath to extract specific elements
node.json_schema.label: JSON Schema
node.json_schema.description: Validate and generate JSON Schema definitions
node.json_schema.hint: Use for data validation and API contract documentation
node.ldap.label: LDAP
node.ldap.hint: Provide LDAP server, credentials, and search criteria
node.log_parser.label: Log Parser
node.log_parser.hint: Define log format patterns and extraction rules
node.matrix.label: Matrix
node.matrix.description: Perform matrix operations and linear algebra
node.matrix.hint: Input matrix dimensions and operation type
node.mqtt.label: MQTT
